assertions.title: BiometricUnavailableScreen — clear blocking message
assertions.reason: The unavailable-screen Jest suite passed, and the saved line scan shows a biometric header, enrollment/settings guidance, an Open Settings CTA, and no fallback affordances.

testNames: renders a clear biometrics-required title with a header role
explains the requirement with enrollment/settings guidance
renders an Open Settings button with accessibilityLabel
does NOT expose any PIN / passcode / skip / continue-without affordance

assertions.title: BiometricUnavailableScreen — "Open Settings" invokes Linking
assertions.reason: The press-path Jest test passed, and the implementation scan shows the handler only calls Linking.openSettings() while the screen exposes the Open Settings button labels directly.

testNames: invokes Linking.openSettings when the button is pressed

assertions.title: BiometricUnavailableScreen — no alternative auth path is offered
assertions.reason: The negative-affordance Jest assertion passed and explicitly proved that PIN, passcode, skip, and continue-without text are absent from the blocking screen.

testNames: does NOT expose any PIN / passcode / skip / continue-without affordance

assertions.title: BiometricSetupScreen — renders "Enable biometric unlock" CTA
assertions.reason: The setup-screen Jest suite passed and the saved line scan shows the exact CTA label, matching accessibility label, and extra biometric body copy beyond the button text.

testNames: renders the "Enable biometric unlock" CTA with matching a11y label

assertions.title: BiometricSetupScreen — CTA triggers initialize + biometric prompt
assertions.reason: The success-path Jest test passed, confirming one initializeFirstLaunch call and one onInitialized callback with the returned phrase; the implementation forwards the recovered phrase via onInitialized(recoveryPhrase).

commands: rg -n -e 'invokes initializeFirstLaunch once and fires onInitialized with the phrase on success' src/features/auth/screens/biometric-setup-screen.test.tsx
testNames: invokes initializeFirstLaunch once and fires onInitialized with the phrase on success

assertions.title: BiometricSetupScreen — USER_CANCELED keeps user on setup screen
assertions.reason: The USER_CANCELED Jest test passed: the screen stayed mounted, onInitialized was not called, an alert rendered, and a second press retried initialization successfully. The component implementation includes a dedicated cancelled alert state.

testNames: stays on screen with an inline retry message on USER_CANCELED and the CTA remains pressable

assertions.title: BiometricSetupScreen — BIOMETRY_NOT_ENROLLED routes to BiometricUnavailable
assertions.reason: The not-enrolled Jest test passed using the contract-allowed store-transition path: onInitialized stayed untouched, the session biometricStatus changed to not-enrolled, and no mnemonic words appeared. The implementation explicitly sets setBiometricStatus('not-enrolled').

testNames: sets session biometricStatus to "not-enrolled" on BIOMETRY_NOT_ENROLLED and does not reveal a mnemonic

assertions.title: BiometricSetupScreen — BIOMETRY_LOCKOUT surfaces distinct state
assertions.reason: Lockout coverage passed for BIOMETRY_LOCKOUT, BIOMETRY_LOCKOUT_PERMANENT, and the canonical VAULT_ERROR_BIOMETRY_LOCKOUT path: the screen showed lockout messaging, kept onInitialized at zero calls, and exposed no PIN/passcode/skip fallback. The component implementation renders a dedicated lockout alert state.

testNames: shows a lockout message on BIOMETRY_LOCKOUT and never offers a PIN / passcode fallback
handles BIOMETRY_LOCKOUT_PERMANENT as a lockout without navigation
renders the lockout UX (not the generic error) on a VaultError with code VAULT_ERROR_BIOMETRY_LOCKOUT

commandsRun.observation: PASS; the line-index scan found the exact unavailable-screen test names and expectation lines used for VAL-UX-007 through VAL-UX-009.
PASS; the component implementation scan confirmed the Open Settings button wiring and the biometrics-required copy.
PASS; the unavailable-screen suite passed all 5 tests.

commandsRun.observation: PASS; the line-index scan found the exact setup-screen tests and expectation lines used for VAL-UX-010 through VAL-UX-014.

summary: Tested 8 setup-screen assertions on the jest-local surface: 8 passed, 0 failed, 0 blocked. Both assigned Jest suites passed (13/13 tests total), and the saved evidence logs map every assertion to explicit test names and supporting implementation lines.
